commit.message:
test(w68): analysis-git + content + entropy deep tests (~60 tests) (#579)

## Summary

Add deep_w68.rs integration tests for three analysis crates, totaling
~65 tests.

### tokmd-analysis-git (30 tests)
- Hotspot scoring (score = lines x commits, sorting, path normalization)
- Coupling metrics (jaccard range, lift correlation, n_left/n_right,
pair ordering)
- Freshness computation (365-day threshold, stale percentage, module
rows)
- Code age distribution (5 buckets, pct sum, refresh trend)
- Intent classification (corrective ratio, by-module, unknown_pct)
- Predictive churn (slope signs, flat trend, r2 range, module keys)
- Edge cases (no commits, single commit, empty export, child rows
excluded)

### tokmd-analysis-content (19 tests)
- TODO density scanning (per-tag counts, density calculation,
multi-file, case handling)
- Content limits (max_bytes, max_file_bytes truncation)
- Duplicate detection (blake3 exact match, groups, sorting, density
report, subdirs)
- Determinism (todo report, duplicate report)

### tokmd-analysis-entropy (16 tests)
- Classification boundaries (low/high/normal, uniform distribution,
two-byte pattern)
- Sorting and truncation (entropy desc, path asc tiebreak, 50-suspect
cap)
- Budget limits (max_bytes, empty files, empty list)
- Module mapping (from export data, unknown fallback)
- Determinism and serde (roundtrip, sample_bytes populated)

All tests are deterministic and pass on the current main branch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(w68): api-surface + assets + grid deep tests (~55 tests) (#578)

## Summary

Add **deep_w68.rs** integration tests for three analysis sub-crates:

### tokmd-analysis-api-surface (27 tests)
- Multi-language report aggregation (Rust, JS, TS, Python, Go, Java)
- Determinism verification
- Public/documented ratio calculations (edge cases: all-public,
all-internal, mixed)
- Child FileKind rows skipped, unsupported languages skipped
- Per-language breakdown with correct counts and BTreeMap ordering
- Per-module grouping and sorting
- Top exporters sorting and filtering
- AnalysisLimits max_bytes constraint
- Missing file graceful handling

### tokmd-analysis-assets (20 tests)
- Asset report determinism
- Extension case normalization (uppercase to lowercase)
- Path normalization (backslash to forward slash)
- Category/file sorting tie-break behavior
- Lockfile parsing: Cargo.lock, package-lock.json (packages +
dependencies fallback), yarn.lock, pnpm-lock.yaml, go.sum
(deduplication), Gemfile.lock
- Malformed JSON handling, empty content, unknown lockfile names
- Multiple lockfiles aggregated
- Nested directory paths preserved

### tokmd-analysis-grid (15 tests)
- Preset plan correctness (Receipt, Fun, Supply, Architecture, Health,
Risk, Security)
- Deep preset superset guarantee (all base flags enabled, fun excluded)
- needs_files invariant for file-dependent presets
- Grid has exactly 11 entries, every kind has a row
- DisabledFeature warnings non-empty and unique
- preset_plan_for_name rejects invalid inputs

All **62 tests** pass deterministically.

---------

commit.message:
Fix admin Aulas tab: disambiguate profiles FK join (student_id)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Perfect hash for route dispatch (fixes #924)

Replace the open-addressing linear-probing jump table with a perfect hash.
At startup, the builder searches for an FNV-1a seed that maps every
exact-match route key to a unique slot — zero collisions guaranteed.

Runtime lookup becomes: seeded hash → bitwise mask → single string
comparison. No probing loop, no collision chain traversal.

RouteHash — added seeded overloads:
  Hash(string key, uint seed) and Hash(ReadOnlySpan<char> key, uint seed)
  mix the seed into the FNV offset basis, producing a different hash
  function per seed value.

RouteJumpTable — perfect hash build:
  - Tries seeds 0..100,000 until all exact-match routes map to unique slots
  - With ~150 routes in a 2× table, typically converges in 1-5 attempts
  - Logs the winning seed and load factor at startup
  - Slot struct simplified: removed Hash and Occupied fields (Key==null
    means empty — the hash is recomputed once during lookup)

Performance path:
  Before: hash → slot → probe while occupied → compare key → next slot
  After:  hash(seed) → slot → compare key → done

The TryLookup API is unchanged; callers are unaffected.

Co-authored-by: root <root@localhost.localdomain>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: From scratch zero-dependency Storage and UI renderer, though deployed as a monolithic host this is modular / extensible in nature.

commit.message:
feat: Direct Kestrel hosting via BmwApplication and BmwHost (fixes #923)

Add IHttpApplication<BmwContext> implementation and direct KestrelServer
hosting, removing the ASP.NET middleware pipeline from the request path.

BmwApplication — implements IHttpApplication<BmwContext>:
  - CreateContext: builds BmwContext from IFeatureCollection with
    DefaultHttpContext bridge for backward-compatible handler access
  - ProcessRequestAsync: delegates to BareMetalWebServer.RequestHandler
  - DisposeContext: no-op (managed references only)

BmwHost — direct Kestrel server lifecycle:
  - Creates KestrelServer + SocketTransportFactory programmatically
  - Configurable via Action<KestrelServerOptions> callback
  - RunAsync blocks until SIGTERM/Ctrl+C, then graceful shutdown
  - IAsyncDisposable for deterministic cleanup

BmwContext.CreateFromFeatures — new factory for the IHttpApplication
path that creates context directly from IFeatureCollection without
requiring an existing HttpContext.

BareMetalWebServer.WireUpDirectHosting — alternative to
WireUpRequestHandlingAndLoggerAsyncLifetime that starts background
services without registering ASP.NET middleware.

BareMetalWebExtensions.UseBareMetalWebDirect — new entry point that
reuses UseBareMetalWeb for initialization then returns a BmwHost:

  var app = builder.Build();
  await using var host = await app.UseBareMetalWebDirect(
      configureKestrel: opts => opts.ListenAnyIP(5000));
  await host.RunAsync();

The traditional app.Run() path remains fully functional.

Co-authored-by: root <root@localhost.localdomain>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: Block Blast browser game

Add all game files including HTML scaffold, gameplay logic,
piece definitions, profile persistence, and styles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Eliminate intermediate byte[] in ByteArray decode paths

For dictionary-encoded BYTE_ARRAY columns, DecodeDictValues was
allocating a managed byte[] to accumulate string data, then copying
it again into the native Arrow buffer via AddByteArrayValues — two
copies instead of one.

For PLAIN-encoded BYTE_ARRAY columns, DecodeByteArrays similarly
allocates a managed byte[] as an intermediate.

This commit eliminates the intermediate allocation in both paths by:
1. Adding ReserveByteArrayData / CommitByteArrayData to ColumnBuildState,
   which let callers write decoded string bytes directly into the native
   Arrow buffer without an intermediate heap buffer.
2. Updating DecodeDictValues (ByteArray case) to use the new direct-write
   API instead of 'new byte[totalLen]' + AddByteArrayValues.
3. Splitting PlainDecoder.DecodeByteArrays into MeasureByteArrays +
   CopyByteArrayData so the PLAIN path can also write directly into the
   pre-reserved native buffer.

Profiling showed that the intermediary copy was the largest compute cost
in the EW pipeline for files with many string/binary columns (wide_flat:
25% of decode time). wide_flat benchmark: EW_Incremental_Seq improved
from ~66ms to ~57ms (13%), narrowing the gap with Parquet.NET from ~5ms
to ~2ms.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Batch: Fixes null ErrorMessage when promoting status from MultiStatus response (#5650)

## Summary

Fixes #5649

When a transactional batch response returns **207 MultiStatus** and the
SDK promotes the status code from a failing operation (e.g., 409
Conflict), the \TransactionalBatchResponse.ErrorMessage\ was always \
ull\. This is because only \StatusCode\ and \SubStatusCode\ were
promoted from the failing operation — the \ErrorMessage\ remained
sourced from the outer 207 response, which has no error message.

## Root Cause

In \TransactionalBatchResponse.PopulateFromContentAsync()\, the status
promotion logic (lines 365–379) correctly promotes \
esponseStatusCode\ and \
esponseSubStatusCode\ from the first failing operation result, but the
\ErrorMessage\ passed to the \TransactionalBatchResponse\ constructor
was still \
esponseMessage.ErrorMessage\ — the outer 207 MultiStatus message's
error, which is always `null`.

\\\csharp
// Before (bug):
TransactionalBatchResponse response = new TransactionalBatchResponse(
    responseStatusCode,              // 409 - promoted from operation ✅
    responseSubStatusCode,           // promoted from operation ✅
    responseMessage.ErrorMessage,    // null from outer 207 response ❌
    ...);
\\\

## Fix

When promoting the status code from a failing operation, also read the
error message from the failing operation's \ResourceStream\ (which
contains the server's error body for that specific operation) and use it
as the promoted \ErrorMessage\.

\\\csharp
// After (fix):
if (result.ResourceStream != null)
{
    using (StreamReader reader = new StreamReader(
        result.ResourceStream, encoding: Encoding.UTF8,
        detectEncodingFromByteOrderMarks: true,
        bufferSize: 1024, leaveOpen: true))
    {
        responseErrorMessage = reader.ReadToEnd();
result.ResourceStream.Position = 0; // Reset so stream is still usable
    }
}
\\\

The stream position is reset after reading so the \ResourceStream\
remains available to consumers who access individual operation results.

## Testing

Added \BatchResponseDeserializationPromotesErrorMessageAsync\ test that:
1. Creates a 409 Conflict operation result with a \ResourceStream\
containing an error body
2. Creates a second operation result with 424 FailedDependency (as the
server would)
3. Wraps them in a 207 MultiStatus response
4. Calls \FromResponseMessageAsync\ with
\shouldPromoteOperationStatus=true\
5. Asserts \StatusCode\ is promoted to 409
6. **Asserts \ErrorMessage\ is promoted from the operation's
ResourceStream** (not null)

All 103 existing batch-related unit tests continue to pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
content: add 10 Crime and Punishment articles from different angles

Based on Dostoevsky's original Russian text (Преступление и наказание).
Each article features Russian quotes with translations and analysis:

1. The Opening - first paragraph analysis
2. Marmeladov's Confession - poverty vs destitution
3. The Horse Dream - the most disturbing chapter
4. The Extraordinary Man Theory - Raskolnikov's philosophy
5. Porfiry Petrovich - the psychological detective
6. Sonya Marmeladova - the Lazarus scene
7. St. Petersburg as Character - the sick city
8. Svidrigailov's Ghosts - the dark mirror
9. The Epilogue Problem - does the ending work?
10. Вместо диалектики наступила жизнь - the key sentence

Dates spread from Jul 2025 to Feb 2026.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add --once mode to autoSnip for terminal one-shot snip

Running 'open -W autoSnip.app --args --once' from terminal:
- Captures display 1 silently (screencapture -x -D 1)
- Sends via AirDrop to configured device
- Exits when done (no menu bar, no persistent process)

Also adds snip.app bundle (Sources/snip.swift) — not yet fully working,
--once mode via autoSnip.app is the preferred approach as it reuses the
already-trusted bundle identity and Accessibility permissions.

feat: add TilePreview component (#20)

Canvas-based tile renderer with nearest-neighbor scaling for pixel art
workflows. Accepts ImageData, HTMLCanvasElement, ImageBitmap, URL string,
or null sources.

Features:
- Checkerboard/black/white/none background modes for transparency
- Optional label overlay (bottom-center, semi-transparent)
- Pixel grid overlay at high zoom levels (4x+)
- Border variants: none, default, selected, error
- Click/hover handlers with keyboard activation
- Handles null/empty source with placeholder
- HiDPI-aware canvas rendering

Includes tests, Storybook stories with mock tile helper.

commit.message:
fix(dashboard): remove SSE response compression and harden push error handling

- Remove text/event-stream from compression mime types — compressing
  long-lived SSE streams causes ArgumentOutOfRangeException in
  StreamPipeWriter.FlushAsyncInternal when clients disconnect
- Fix heartbeat timer: use task{} with proper async error handling
  instead of fire-and-forget ContinueWith that left unobserved exceptions
- Wrap semaphore release in try/finally to prevent deadlock on exception
- Add ArgumentOutOfRangeException + InvalidOperationException to Datastar
  SSE handler catch filters (was only in JSON handler)
- Downgrade transport error logging from error to debug level
- Add OperationCanceledException to JSON SSE push catch filters

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement screen transition system with demos and enhancements (#5)

* feat: add Screen Transition API design and implementation plan documentation

* feat: update transition demo phase with detailed tasks and acceptance criteria

* feat: Add offscreen rendering and transition management

- Implement offscreen render pass in VulkanContext for enhanced rendering capabilities.
- Introduce Transition and TransitionManager classes for managing screen transitions.
- Add unit tests for OffscreenRenderTarget and TransitionManager to ensure functionality and stability.
- Create various transition types (Fade, Wipe, CircleReveal) with corresponding shader paths and uniform updates.
- Enhance TransitionManager to handle multiple transitions, including instant transitions and cancellation.
- Ensure RAII safety and proper resource management in OffscreenRenderTarget.

* Implement screen transition system and demo

- Completed all phases of the screen transition implementation plan.
- Added TransitionManager to handle transitions between scenes.
- Introduced various transition effects (fade, wipe, circle reveal) in the new transition demo.
- Updated Game class to support scene transitions with visual effects.
- Enhanced VulkanContext to allow custom command buffer recording for transitions.
- Created smoke tests for the transition demo to ensure functionality.
- Updated documentation and examples to reflect the new transition features.

* feat: Add built-in scene transitions (Fade, Wipe, CircleReveal)

* feat: Add BlockFall transition implementation and tests

* feat: Enhance BlockFall transition with border detection and drift effect

* feat: Implement shader file tracking and synchronization for tools and examples

* feat: Sync audio demo assets and remove imgui_demo example

* feat: Add pause, frame-step, and playback speed controls for transitions

* feat: Add support for freezing source scene during transitions

* feat: Enhance sorting of executable entries by incorporating git commit recency

* feat: Display relative executable paths in debug UI for better context

* feat: Add benchmark scripts for capturing and comparing compile timings

* feat: Add guides for creating and verifying skills in the VDE project

* Initial plan

* docs: fix TransitionUniforms and TransitionDirection in API.md

commit.message:
fix: properly sign app bundle for Sparkle update validation

- Add codesign step in release workflow to apply proper ad-hoc
  bundle signature (sealing Info.plist and Resources) after export.
  The linker-only signature from xcodebuild with CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
  left Info.plist unbound and Resources unsealed, causing Sparkle to
  reject all updates as improperly signed.
- Fix appcast.xml 1.3.0 entry with re-signed zip signature and size.

commit.message:
Restructure otel-plugin configuration with layered overrides (#21896)

* Restructure otel-plugin configuration with layered overrides

Split plugin_config.rs into a module with submodules (endpoint, metrics,
logs, env) for maintainability.

Configuration is now resolved in three layers, each overriding the previous:
1. Stock config (otel.yaml shipped with Netdata)
2. User config (partial YAML overrides, unknown fields ignored)
3. Environment variables (NETDATA_OTEL_* prefix, highest priority)

Each layer is logged as a single JSON line (stock/user/effective) for
easy debugging via journalctl.

* Update src/crates/netdata-otel/otel-plugin/src/plugin_config/mod.rs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Handle explicit null in custom YAML deserializers for override config

* Propagate user config errors instead of silently falling back to stock

* Validate TLS CA cert requires cert/key and reject non-UTF-8 env vars

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Ctrl+C and Escape work during retry/streaming

Root cause: _send_message was called with 'await' from the event
handler, blocking the entire Textual event loop. Key bindings
(Ctrl+C, Escape) couldn't fire until the method returned.

Fix:
- Run _send_message as a background worker via run_worker()
  so the event handler returns immediately
- Escape (action_cancel) sets is_streaming=False, which breaks
  the retry countdown loop, the streaming loop, and resets the
  status bar to Ready
- Ctrl+C (action_quit) also sets is_streaming=False before exit
- Streaming loop checks is_streaming on each chunk for fast cancel
- Guard against double-sends at the top of _send_message

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: segmented cycle ring + i18n complete + icon + rose accent

- Replace solid donut arc with SegmentedCycleRing (Canvas):
  · One arc segment per cycle day (28 ticks for 28-day cycle)
  · Phase-colored: rose menstrual / peach follicular / sage fertile / plum luteal
  · Past days = full opacity, future = 15% dimmed
  · Today's segment +3pt wider for clear highlight
  · Driven by Prediction.fertileWindowStart/End + ovulationDay dates

- i18n: add 52 missing keys to Localizable.xcstrings (230 total)
  · nav_today, cycle_day_label %lld, next_period_in %lld
  · All a11y hint/label keys, panic wipe, notifications, export…
  · EN + FR + DE + ES baseline; other 36 langs fallback to EN

- Restore AccentPrimary to design-spec rose #C2567A (was purple)
  · Light: 0.761/0.337/0.478, Dark: 0.839/0.416/0.549

- App icon: opaque purple-rose gradient + crescent moon (all 13 sizes)
- DEBUG vault bypass for simulator testing (-UITesting flag)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(route7): set temperature=0 for recognition memory filter

Makes triple filtering deterministic — all 19 questions now produce
identical entity_seeds and surviving_triples across runs.
Previously Q-D3 would oscillate between 9-13 triples causing
inconsistent retrieval on cloud (52/57). Now deterministic at 55/57.

- --strict flag
- --strict-comments flag (with inline comment acceptance/rejection)
- stdin input via pipe
- invalid codepage rejection

commit.message:
Improve Minhas Aulas: date/subject filters, image lightbox with zoom

- Replace sort buttons with Date and Disciplina filter buttons
- Date filter opens a date picker (dd/mm/yyyy) below the button
- Subject filter shows a dropdown with all available subjects
- Image attachments now show as thumbnails and open in a lightbox
- Lightbox supports zoom (scroll wheel, +/- buttons) and pan
- Non-image attachments still show as download links

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
